canonical_definition: A large-scale video dataset for action recognition that focuses on fine-grained human-object interactions, often requiring temporal reasoning.
papers: Scaling Video-Language Models to 10K Frames via Hierarchical Differential Distillation
Unifying Specialized Visual Encoders for Video Language Models

definitions.text: A large-scale video dataset for action recognition that focuses on fine-grained human-object interactions, often requiring temporal reasoning.
definitions.paper: Scaling Video-Language Models to 10K Frames via Hierarchical Differential Distillation

relationships.paper: Scaling Video-Language Models to 10K Frames via Hierarchical Differential Distillation
relationships.evidence: For the action recognition task, we incorporate two baselines: Adaframe (Wu et al., 2019) and MGSampler (Zhi et al., 2021), and evaluate them alongside VILAMP on three benchmarks: ActivityNet (Caba Heilbron et al., 2015), Something-Something V2 (Sth-V2) (Goyal et al., 2017), and UCF-101 (Soomro et al., 2012). (Section 5.4)
